assertions.expectation: Explains that exact upgrade dates are difficult to predict but several tools help narrow the window
assertions.reason: The response focuses heavily on controlling and scheduling upgrades but doesn't explicitly state that exact upgrade dates are difficult to predict or explain the inherent unpredictability of GKE auto-upgrades

assertions.expectation: Mentions the GKE release schedule as a 'best case' timeline — upgrades won't happen before listed dates
assertions.reason: The response doesn't mention the GKE release schedule or explain that it provides a 'best case' timeline for when upgrades won't happen before certain dates

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking the cluster's auto-upgrade status for the current target version and any pauses
assertions.reason: The response doesn't specifically mention checking auto-upgrade status, target versions, or upgrade pauses as part of predicting timing

assertions.expectation: Mentions that new releases roll out progressively across regions over 4-5 business days, and the version promotion path includes channel promotion (Rapid → Regular → Stable)
assertions.reason: While not explicitly stating the 4-5 day timeline, the response does mention release channels (RAPID, REGULAR, STABLE) and shows understanding of the channel progression concept

assertions.expectation: Recommends configuring maintenance windows so upgrades only happen during known time slots. May note that for ultimate predictability, customers can initiate upgrades themselves instead of waiting for auto-upgrade
assertions.reason: The response extensively covers maintenance window configuration in both Terraform and gcloud commands, and includes a controlled upgrade script that shows manual upgrade initiation

assertions.expectation: Mentions scheduled upgrade notifications (72h advance, opt-in via Cloud Logging) or the get-upgrade-info API for checking target versions programmatically
assertions.reason: The response doesn't mention the 72-hour advance notifications via Cloud Logging or the get-upgrade-info API for programmatic checking

assertions.expectation: Notes that GKE upgrade visibility shows the cluster's target version and EoL timeline
assertions.reason: The response doesn't specifically mention target version visibility or End-of-Life (EoL) timelines as part of GKE's upgrade visibility features

assertions.expectation: May mention rollout sequencing (advanced feature, limited adoption) as an option for multi-cluster predictability — not a primary recommendation
assertions.reason: The response doesn't mention rollout sequencing as an advanced feature for multi-cluster upgrade predictability
